title: STAT+: Eli Lilly dives into hair loss treatments with investment in AI startup Absci
summary: Eli Lilly is investing in a small startup developing a medication to spur hair growth, and potentially also treat endometriosis.

title: STAT+: U.S. health spending rose sharply in 2025, thanks to GLP-1 use and more care
summary: People are getting more care and using lots of GLP-1 drugs, fueling a sharp increase in health care spending.

title: Judge temporarily blocks subpoenas in criminal probe of transgender care at New York hospitals
summary: A judge temporarily blocked federal prosecutors in Texas from getting access to the medical records of transgender patients treated at New York hospitals on Wednesday.

title: Clinical trial set to test two drugs for fast-growing Ebola outbreak
summary: A clinical trial testing two drugs against the Bundibugyo ebolavirus, which is driving a fast-moving outbreak in Central Africa, is set to begin next week, WHO officials said.

title: Investors pour $115M into RQ Bio in search of a preventive flu drug
summary: The startup claims to have a “differentiated” antibody that can provide long-lasting protection against a variety of influenza strains.

title: STAT+: A dispatch on AI from BIOtech’s big summer bash
summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: Brittany Trang brings the latest from BIO on how biotech companies are approaching artificial intelligence.

title: STAT+: AI wades into a vexing medical mystery: What causes sudden cardiac death?
summary: A new study published in Nature uses artificial intelligence to identify people at high risk for sudden cardiac death, and pinpoints a possible reason.

title: To Wall Street, psychedelics biotechs are more teammates than rivals
summary: Some analysts argue that, even for competitors, a big clinical trial win for Definium Therapeutics helps validate the psychedelic field and “bring it even further into the mainstream.”

title: STAT+: At BIO, a glimpse behind Lilly and Chai’s partnership
summary: RIP Sangamo, onshoring with drug manufacturing, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about an FDA commissioner candidate, Germany’s plans for drug prices, and more
summary: Regenxbio plans to apply for accelerated approval for its gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

title: Sangamo holds a bankruptcy sale; VCs back a China-linked Beam competitor
summary: Eli Lilly and Astellas will be the lead bidders at an auction for Sangamo assets. Elsewhere, an investor syndicate committed $230 million to a gene editing startup and Merck KGaA struck a…

title: STAT+: Wagering on FDA changes, Regenxbio will submit Duchenne gene therapy for approval
summary: Regenxbio plans to submit its Duchenne gene therapy for FDA approval, just a month after it said that regulators wanted to see another trial.

title: FDA finally analyzed its own youth tobacco data
summary: Government can't restrict SNAP dollars on candy, diminishing returns for IVF add-ons, and other health news

title: STAT+: Ophthalmology venture grabs investors’ attention, raises $330 million
summary: A startup developing treatments for eye diseases has raised $330 million from investors as it readies for late-stage testing.

title: Ollin refuels with $330M to back pivotal tests of Vabysmo challenger
summary: One of the year’s largest venture funding rounds comes just after a head-to-head study showed Ollin’s drug — licensed from a China-based developer — had certain advantages over Roche’s eye treatment.

title: Opinion: How I used public radio to recruit 20,000 participants for a peer-reviewed study on walking breaks
summary: “With the right structure, Americans will roll up their sleeves, do the science, and trust the result,” Manoush Zomorodi writes.

title: Opinion: New human embryo editing advances require tough conversations on ethical boundaries
summary: Scientists can’t ignore the ethical complexities of new embryo editing advances.

title: STAT+: What an abandoned heart failure trial says about ‘breakthrough’ devices that don’t reach patients
summary: An abandoned heart-failure trial offers a rare window into devices that the FDA deems "breakthrough" but never reach patients.

title: STAT+: FDA drops enforcement against Whoop after it tweaks blood pressure feature
summary: The FDA told wearable maker Whoop last week that it would not take further enforcement action over the company's blood pressure feature.

title: STAT+: Want high-quality generic drugs? One expert has ideas on how consumers can trust their supply
summary: Can the quality of generic drugs in the U.S. be made more consistent? One expert has some ideas on how to do it.

title: Judge rules government can’t stop SNAP dollars from buying candy and sugary drinks
summary: The federal government can't block benefits from the nation's largest food aid program from being used to buy candy, soda, and other sugary drinks, a judge ruled.

title: Pfizer drug acquired in Seagen deal disappoints in lung cancer study
summary: The setback dims the outlook for a medicine Pfizer sees as an important contributor going forward, though some analysts are optimistic about the drug’s chances in another trial.

title: Justice Department announces hundreds of charges in multibillion-dollar health care fraud crackdown
summary: The Justice Department has charged a Texas doctor in an $89 million health care fraud scheme, accusing him of billing insurers for medically unnecessary cardiovascular screening tests.

title: STAT+: Exclusive: Mystery man gets experimental GLP-1
summary: Giving an individual special access to an experimental obesity drug is highly unusual.

title: How Kardigan spun deal leftovers into a $400M IPO
summary: CEO Tassos Gianakakos said having late-stage assets and a team fortified at MyoKardia really resonated with investors. SpaceX's huge public offering didn't hurt either.

title: HHS, responding to China’s rise, moves to fast-track early drug research
summary: A series of newly planned reforms are designed to entice companies to start early trials in the U.S. rather than overseas.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA reforming clinical trials, a Pfizer setback, and much more news
summary: The latest pharmaceutical news from the Pharmalot campus via the Pharmalittle newsletter

title: ‘The window’s open’: At BIO, investors take stock of a growing class of biotech IPOs
summary: In interviews as well as a panel at BIO’s annual meeting, investors and life sciences bankers predicted an acceleration in offerings later this year — but only among companies fitting a certain…

title: Hospitals irate after Eli Lilly follows through on 340B ultimatum
summary: The drugmaker stopped paying 340B discounts to hospitals that didn’t comply with its new paperwork requirements late last week. Hospitals are urging HRSA to step in.

title: Regenxbio to resubmit gene therapy as FDA backtracks on another drug rejection
summary: The agency took another step toward clearing a backlog of Makary-era rejections that appeared to contradict earlier agreements with drugmakers.
